creative_brief: Close-up of a hand turning over generic supermarket meat packaging, label deliberately blurred and anonymous. Contrast with a second frame showing a handwritten Bundarra Farm label on butcher paper. Warm, natural light. No text overlay needed.
primary_text:
Last Tuesday, a mum in Brookvale turned over a pack of mince. No farm name. No region. Just a barcode and a use-by date.

She didn't know where it came from. She didn't know how far it travelled. She fed it to her kids anyway, because what else do you do?

That feeling: it shouldn't be normal. Knowing your farmer shouldn't be a luxury.

FarmThru connects you to farms like Bundarra and Little Yarran. Real names, real places, real food. No middlemen. No warehouses. Order when you want, collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

This is becoming something bigger than a grocery store. You can own a piece of it.
headline: She couldn't name a single farm
description: Farm-direct groceries from regenerative NSW farms. Be part of what's growing.
